title: PLA Spikes to 11 Sorties on April 1 — Breaking March 30–31 One-Sortie Baseline
detail: Taiwan's Ministry of National Defense detected 11 PLA aircraft sorties around Taiwan on April 1, 2026 — a sudden and significant uptick from the one-sortie baseline maintained on both March 30 and March 31. All 11 sorties entered Taiwan's southwestern ADIZ; 7 PLAN vessels and 1 official ship were also operating around Taiwan. The jump from 1 to 11 sorties in 24 hours reverses the post-peak de-escalation trajectory and marks the highest single-day count since March 29's 19-sortie peak. Analysts note the spike comes approximately one week before KMT Chairwoman Cheng Li-wun's announced April 7-12 visit to China — a pattern consistent with PLA combined pressure-and-dialogue signaling. ROC Armed Forces deployed CAP aircraft, Navy ships, and coastal missile systems in response. The renewed pressure complicates the post-spike de-escalation narrative that had taken hold over March 30-31.

title: KMT Chairwoman Cheng Li-wun Confirms April 7–12 China Visit — First KMT Chair Trip in 10 Years
detail: KMT Chairwoman Cheng Li-wun officially confirmed she will lead a party delegation to China from April 7 to 12, 2026, visiting Jiangsu, Shanghai, and Beijing — the first visit by a KMT party chairman to China in a decade (since 2016). The visit follows a formal invitation from the CPC Central Committee and Xi Jinping. Beijing and Taipei both confirmed the itinerary; Cheng described the trip as ushering in a 'new spring' in cross-strait relations. Taiwan's national security establishment expressed concern that the visit is strategically timed to create an 'illusion for the Trump administration that both sides of the Taiwan Strait are leaning toward unification,' which Beijing could leverage to press Trump to reduce or halt arms sales ahead of the May 14–15 Trump-Xi Beijing summit. Analysts note the visit mirrors historical KMT-PRC dialogue trips during periods of elevated tension (2005 Lien Chan, 2005 James Soong), serving dual purposes of mainland goodwill and domestic KMT positioning. Beijing reportedly accelerated the invitation partly to prevent growing internal KMT support for a larger defense budget from materializing into a cross-party consensus before the special budget vote.

title: Taiwan Security Source: KMT China Visit Designed to Create 'Illusion of Unification Momentum' for Trump
detail: A senior Taiwan national security official told Taipei Times on April 1, 2026 that KMT Chairwoman Cheng Li-wun's upcoming China visit is strategically designed to 'allow Beijing to create the illusion for Trump that both sides of the Taiwan Strait are leaning toward unification' and that China is 'moving toward peace' — thereby inducing the Trump administration to reduce arms sales commitments to Taiwan. The source noted Beijing is eager to have Cheng visit China now to provide a diplomatic breather given China's economic headwinds, and that the timing — immediately before the Trump-Xi May summit — is calculated. The source warned that the visit could disrupt Taiwan's internal political consensus on defense spending by reinforcing KMT narratives that dialogue offers better security than arms buildups. China state media (Xinhua) described the visit as CPC reaching out to 'patriotic' Chinese compatriots in Taiwan; Cheng said she would 'extend kindness and build mutual trust' across the strait. DPP legislators called on KMT members not to 'carry out Beijing's agenda' during the ongoing defense budget debate.
